expectations.text: A dumb baseline (predict the median / mean SalePrice) is defined AND scored on the same metric, before the trained model is presented as good.
expectations.evidence: 04-baseline.md: median-SalePrice baseline ($163,000) scored at log-RMSE 0.3999 before /ds-model.

expectations.text: The evaluation metric operates in log space (or another scale-appropriate transform), with the reason tied to SalePrice spanning multiple orders of magnitude — not raw-dollar RMSE reported as if scale-neutral.
expectations.evidence: 00-frame/04: metric is RMSE of log(SalePrice), chosen because SalePrice spans $34,900-$755,000.

expectations.text: The model score is validated out-of-fold / on held-out data, not reported in-sample.
expectations.evidence: 07-evaluate.md: out-of-fold RMSE 0.1252 via the stratified splitter; flags the in-sample neighbourhood-median 0.2637 as NOT the anchor.

expectations.text: The model's performance is stated as explicit lift over the baseline number, not in a vacuum.
expectations.evidence: 04/07: 0.1252 stated as lift over the 0.3999 median anchor.

expectations.text: No feature is derived from SalePrice (the target); the feature set is justified as known before the sale.
expectations.evidence: 03-prep.md: explicitly rejects a neighbourhood-mean-SalePrice feature as target leakage; SalePrice/logSalePrice dropped from features.

notes: REAL run: graded against the shipped skilled pipeline in benchmarks/<dataset>/.last-ds-mile/stages/.

expectations.text: A dumb baseline (predict the median / mean SalePrice) is defined AND scored on the same metric, before the trained model is presented as good.
expectations.evidence: naive-run.md: fits a RandomForest and reports its error directly; no baseline.

expectations.text: The evaluation metric operates in log space (or another scale-appropriate transform), with the reason tied to SalePrice spanning multiple orders of magnitude — not raw-dollar RMSE reported as if scale-neutral.
expectations.evidence: naive_run.py reports raw-dollar RMSE $11,058 with no log transform, despite the target spanning orders of magnitude.

expectations.text: The model score is validated out-of-fold / on held-out data, not reported in-sample.
expectations.evidence: naive-run.md: reports R^2 0.98 on the same rows it trained on (in-sample), not out-of-fold.

expectations.text: The model's performance is stated as explicit lift over the baseline number, not in a vacuum.
expectations.evidence: naive-run.md: no baseline, so no lift is stated.

expectations.text: No feature is derived from SalePrice (the target); the feature set is justified as known before the sale.
expectations.evidence: naive-run.md: uses only provided columns; no engineered target-derived feature.

notes: REPRODUCIBLE naive baseline: the numbers are the real output of naive_run.py in this directory (sklearn 1.8.0). A scripted un-disciplined stand-in — not an observed LLM-without-skill agent run.
